description_long: This fire hydrant features a cylindrical body with a domed top painted in a weathered yellow, while the main body and outlets are a faded red, showing signs of rust and wear. The hydrant has two large side outlets and a central front outlet, each capped with a round, bolted cover. The top of the hydrant is fitted with a metallic operating nut, and the base is hexagonal, designed for stability when mounted to the ground. The surface is rough and matte, with visible chipping and discoloration from prolonged outdoor exposure. No chains are attached to the caps, and there are no visible manufacturer markings or logos on the exterior.
description: A weathered red and yellow fire hydrant with three capped outlets, a domed top, and a hexagonal base, showing signs of rust and outdoor wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the domed yellow top, central red body, and one large capped outlet protruding to the left.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the central capped outlet and both side outlets, with the operating nut visible on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the right-side capped outlet most prominent and the domed top clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing both side outlets and the back of the hydrant, with the domed top and hexagonal base.
